name: Minoa
startIntroPart1: The lilies and the bliss wash over you oh Priestesses, your people have some offerings, your Goddess said, this is a proud kingdom of traders and artisans centred on the island of Crete, away from the petty and bloody squabbles of the main Lander around you. In this perpetual Mediterranean bliss and Peace your people blossomed, becoming the most culturally and technologically advanced empire of the age. The tales of your prosperity spread across the isles of the Aegean through your many trade routes and yet somehow you priestesses remain forgotten in the palaces.
startIntroPart2: Oh Priestesses, The Minoan empire has long since fallen, your Goddess is gone, yet men continue to sing tales of glory to which your kingdom was the setting without singing also your names. It is time to raise that kingdom anew and reclaim your names! Will you spur your merchants and civilians to rebuild their ships and palaces, reclaiming Crete? Will your palaces and pottery once again be the envy of all the nations around you? Will you build a civilization that will stand the test of time?
declaringWar: You have disrespected the sacred grounds in our palaces! the snakes, the curses, and your head, shall be severed and be eaten.
attacked: War? Huh, be careful for what you wished for. Minoans are not as merciful as we seems to be, you know.
defeated: Oh, Mother Goddess, How could you let us stumble. Hear our Weeping, oh, Snake Goddess, Please do not let Minoa fall!
introduction: We here by the permission and blessings of the Snake Goddess, and I as Chief Priestess of all Minoa, welcomes you to the palace of Knossos. Ladies prepare a banquet!
neutralLetsHearIt: Can we perhaps assist in something?
neutralNo: Perhaps, but, not exactly what we're looking for.
neutralYes: May Mother Goddess bless your land bountiful.
The Snake Goddess is very pleased.
hateHello: Greetings, The chief priestess is very busy, I shall entertain you instead.
hateNo: Is our meeting today, a jest to you?
afterPeace: Really? Sure, It's just a shame that the Snake Goddess doesn't have the chance to taste your soul and skull.
tradeRequest: Olive oil, pottery, fishes, and what ever you desire, we have. I've also heard the things that you have and would you kindly enlighten us about it.
denounced: We did not know what accusations you're pointing at. but hear this, the curses will fall, no matter what your mouth is spewing out.
declaringfriendship: The Mother Goddess is very pleased with you and your people, so is The Snake Goddess. Please, if we may, let both of our people live as companions.
uniqueText: The Minoans Starts the game with [Sailing]. The Minoans got +50% more yield from every [Fishing Boats] in [Coastal] cities, The Minoans also got +70% more yield from farm built in [Coastal] tiles while [Plantation] in [Coastal] tiles got +60% more yield. 38% Food will be carried over after population increases in all cities connected to capital. If [Harbor] is constructed in all [Coastal] cities, each Trade Route will begun to produce [+3 Culture], +3 [Food], and +3 [Gold].
uniques: [38]% Food is carried over after population increases [in all cities connected to capital]
[+3 Culture, +3 Food, +3 Gold] from each Trade Route <if [Harbor] is constructed in all [Coastal] cities>
